name: Flint Hills Beef Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 pound boneless beef sirloin steak, cut into 1-inch thick pieces
3/4 cup small red bell pepper, cubed
1/2 cup red onion, halved and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
For the dressing: In a screw-top jar, combine the olive oil, tarragon vinegar, Dijon mustard, dried tarragon, and salt. Cover and shake well. Set aside.
Slash the fat on edges of the steak at 1-inch intervals. Place the steak on an unheated rack in a broiler pan. Broil 3-4 inches from the heat to desired doneness, turning once. Allow 10-12 minutes for Medium-Rare, or 12-15 minutes for medium. Cut the meat into 1-inch cubes.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the beef, cauliflower, broccoli, red bell pepper, yellow bell pepper, onion, and bacon. Toss to mix well. Shake the dressing and pour it over the beef mixture. Toss to coat. Arrange on a dinner plate over lettuce. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Makes 4 main-dish servings.
Per serving: 405 calories, 27 grams fat, 8 grams carbs, 83 mg cholesterol, 398 mg sodium.
